name: Tree Path Problems — Path Sum, Max Path, Prefix Technique

metadata.career_relevance: Path Sum III (prefix+map in tree) is a canonical O(n) technique — same as SubarrayK but on trees. Max Path Sum is a Google hard. These test ability to combine DFS with HashMap.

hook.real_world_problem:
// Path Sum III: O(n²) naive → O(n) with prefix sum + HashMap
// Same trick as SubarrayK, applied to trees
hook.what_you_will_build: Path Sum I/II/III, Max Path, Good Nodes, Sum Root-to-Leaf Numbers.

concept_explanation.path_sum_iii: Prefix sum + HashMap in DFS. Backtrack: restore map after each subtree.

key_takeaways: Path Sum: subtract target at each node; check ==0 at leaf
Path Sum II: backtrack — add to path, recurse, then remove last
Max Path Sum: max(0, child) clips negatives; update global with l+node+r
Path Sum III: prefix HashMap in tree DFS; put(0,1); backtrack map
Sum root-to-leaf: cur=cur*10+node.val; add at leaf
Good Nodes: pass maxSoFar down; good if node >= maxSoFar
reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: Trie — prefix tree for autocomplete and word search.
